props.title: Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) | Maryville University
props.description: Earn your BSN online at Maryville. CCNE-accredited, transfer-friendly, with a direct pathway into MSN and DNP study. Prepare for the NCLEX-RN and a leadership-ready nursing career.

props.heading: Bachelor of Science in Nursing
props.subheading: Build the clinical foundation and leadership mindset the next decade of healthcare demands. CCNE accredited. Transfer-friendly. Stackable into MSN and DNP.

props.body: The Institute of Medicine called for 80% of the U.S. nursing workforce to hold a BSN. Magnet hospitals require it. Employers increasingly require it. Beyond credentialing, the BSN builds the research, leadership, and population-health competencies that separate nurses who follow the care plan from nurses who shape it.
props.heading: Why the Bachelor of Science in Nursing Matters Now

features.heading: 100% Online Coursework
features.description: Didactic content delivered fully online on your schedule.

features.heading: Local Clinical Placements
features.description: Complete clinical hours in your own community.

features.heading: Direct-Entry Pathways
features.description: Stackable credits into MSN and DNP programs.

features.heading: No GRE Required
features.description: Application focuses on academics and goals, not test scores.

features.heading: Six Starts Per Year
features.description: Begin when you're ready — six cohorts annually.

features.heading: Practicing Faculty
features.description: Every instructor is a practicing clinician.

props.sectionHeading: Bachelor of Science in Nursing Program Highlights

props.body: The BSN sequences foundational arts, sciences, and general education (NURS 307 Health Assessment, NURS 309 Pharmacology, NURS 330 Pathophysiology) alongside nursing-specific coursework (NURS 341 Med-Surg, NURS 342 Maternal/Child, NURS 406 Community Health, NURS 416 Leadership). Students complete 120 total credits, with clinical placements integrated throughout the upper-division sequence.
props.heading: BSN Curriculum Overview

testimonials.name: Joe B.
testimonials.quote: I feel like Maryville is really helping to prepare me for my career path.

testimonials.name: Tiffany N.
testimonials.quote: Each semester built upon the previous one, so we're learning as we go.

testimonials.name: Celena S.
testimonials.quote: It's never going to be a perfect time — just step out and do it.

items.answer: The BSN is for students pursuing their first nursing credential. The RN to BSN is a completion program for RNs who hold an associate degree or diploma in nursing and already have an active RN license.
items.question: What's the difference between the BSN and RN to BSN?

items.answer: Full-time students typically complete the BSN in four years. The timeline can flex based on transfer credits and course load.
items.question: How long does the BSN take?

items.answer: Yes. The BSN is designed to prepare you for the NCLEX-RN licensure exam. Maryville's pass rates exceed national averages.
items.question: Will I be prepared for the NCLEX?

items.answer: Maryville's clinical placement team partners with hospitals and health systems nationwide and works with you to secure placements in your community.
items.question: Are clinical placements arranged for me?

items.answer: Yes. Many students begin part-time while completing prerequisites or balancing work/family. An enrollment advisor can map a pacing plan.
items.question: Can I start part-time?

props.heading: Start Your BSN Today
props.description: Speak with an enrollment advisor today. No pressure, no fees — just answers.
